title: Transport Protocols, Ports, and Network Host Services · Changed-Device Lab
summary: A different user, device, environment, symptom, constraint, and changed condition requires an independent transfer of transport protocols, ports, and network host services into a new transport and service evidence map. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of transport and service evidence map with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Contrast connection-oriented and connectionless transport, ports, sockets, sessions, reliability, ordering, flow, latency, and application responsibility.
Use current registries to recognize common service-port conventions while distinguishing registered, configured, observed, proxied, and tunneled behavior.
Trace web, mail, file, directory, remote-access, time, address, name, authentication, print, and database service dependencies.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# comptia-a-plus cumulative fictional support evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic text-contract review only. They never execute learner
# commands, inspect the host, touch devices or accounts, connect to networks, open equipment,
# handle malware, erase media, contact people, or claim certification.
workspace: support
ticket-id: fictional-ticket
user-impact: [replace with user outcome, scope, severity, and urgency]
environment: [replace with asset, version, configuration, owner, dependency, and evidence time]
safety-authority: [replace with consent, hazards, data custody, privacy, access, and stop rules]
baseline: [replace with expected behavior, known-good state, conditions, and measurement]
symptom: [replace with observed behavior, reproduction, frequency, timing, and evidence]
recent-change: [replace with timeline, relevant changes, counterevidence, and unknowns]
hypothesis: [replace with falsifiable cause, competing explanation, prediction, and confidence]
test: [replace with one safe reversible discriminating test, changed case, and stop rule]
observation: [replace with actual result, provenance, rejected inference, and next decision]
repair-rollback: [replace with least-change repair, authority, backup, trigger, and restored state]
verify: [replace with user outcome, original, changed, side-effect, restart, and recurrence checks]
prevention: [replace with prevention, monitoring, owner, review date, and residual risk]
escalation: [replace with stop threshold, destination, evidence packet, and requested decision]
documentation: [replace with plain structured status, redaction, access, handoff, and uncertainty]
transfer-boundary: authorized disposable equipment or simulator only
review:

steps.title: CompTIA A+ support Commit to the mission model · Lab Transport Behavior
steps.instruction:
lab case — Web content partly loads, mail sends but does not receive, and a firewall log uses familiar ports that do not prove the actual service. The lab variation 2 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Contrast connection-oriented and connectionless transport, ports, sockets, sessions, reliability, ordering, flow, latency, and application responsibility.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: CompTIA A+ support Commit to the mission model · Lab Transport Behavior.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for contrast connection-oriented and connectionless transport, ports, sockets, sessions, reliability, ordering, flow, latency, and application responsibility. For CompTIA A+ support Commit to the mission model · Lab Transport Behavior, keep Transport Behavior connected to mechanically reviewable user-impact, asset, safety, baseline, symptom, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, escalation, documentation, and transfer decision evidence.

steps.title: CompTIA A+ support Test an acceptance boundary · Lab Bounded Service Test
steps.instruction:
lab case — Web content partly loads, mail sends but does not receive, and a firewall log uses familiar ports that do not prove the actual service.
